web_results.title: Nine Men's Morris Strategy | Tips, Tactics & Openings
web_results.snippet: Nine Men's Morris Strategy: Tips, Tactics & Openings Nine Men's Morris is a classic two-player abstract strategy board game dating back to the Roman Empire. Form mills, three pieces in a row, to remove your opponent's pieces. The player who reduces their opponent to two pieces or blocks all their moves wins. This ancient game combines simple rules with deep strategic possibilities.

web_results.title: Nine men's morris - Wikipedia
web_results.snippet: Nine men's morris is a solved game, that is, a game whose optimal strategy has already been calculated. It has been shown that with perfect play from both players, the game results in a draw.

web_results.snippet: You can't guarantee a win. web_results.title: PDF Nine Men's Morris Cube Algorithms - langnotes.com
web_results.snippet: Introduction Nine Men's Morris is an abstract strategy board game for two players that emerged from the Roman Empire. The game is also known as Nine Man Morris, Mill, Mills, Merels, Merelles, and Merrills in English. Each player has nine pieces, or "men", which move among the board's twenty-four intersections. The object of the game is to leave the opposing player with fewer than three pieces ...

web_results.title: Nine Men's Morris: Rules and Strategies — Mike Mabey
web_results.snippet: Here are some things I really like about Nine Men's Morris: The rules are simple, so teaching it to a friend doesn't take very long. Even my three-year-old understands the mechanics and enjoys a game with me or his siblings. While it does require some strategy, it doesn't require the same level of foresight, planning, and brainpower as chess.

web_results.snippet: Nine Men's Morris, also known as Merrills, Mill and Merels, is an old English game that goes back to antiquity - evidence has been found for the game in ancient Rome and possibly Ancient Egypt. More recently, references in the works of Shakespeare have given it an image of the archetypal medieval board game.
